savage_analysis.brutal_summary: This command library has more complexity than a tax code and less reliability than a 1970s Yugo. 22.4% average failure rate? That's not 'intelligent automation', that's expensive randomness.

specific_roasts.roast: 672 lines of 'intelligent' prompts with 35% failure rate. This isn't AI enhancement, it's computational self-harm. You've built a Rube Goldberg machine for thinking.
specific_roasts.evidence: 8.5 complexity score with 15 thinking blocks. That's 44.7 lines per thinking block - clearly someone confused 'comprehensive' with 'comprehensible'.

specific_roasts.roast: 16 MCP dependencies for a 'hybrid' solution. That's not hybrid, that's a dependency Jenga tower waiting to collapse. 42% failure rate proves it.
specific_roasts.evidence: 9.0 complexity score with σ=1.73 deviation. This is 2.3 standard deviations above average - statistically an outlier, practically a nightmare.

specific_roasts.roast: Actually decent. 15% failure rate and readable structure. Proof that focusing on one thing well beats trying to solve the universe.
specific_roasts.evidence: 6.0 complexity with 7/10 maintainability. This is what happens when ADHD hyperfocus meets actual engineering discipline.

specific_roasts.roast: Best in class with 8% failure rate. Ironic that the 'boring' TDD command is the most reliable. Almost like proven methodologies work better than AI poetry.
specific_roasts.evidence: 459 lines, 12 thinking blocks, 9/10 maintainability. This is what engineering looks like when you stop trying to be clever.

specific_roasts.roast: Finally, someone understood that 'rapid' means less code, not more features. 249 lines that actually work 88% of the time.
specific_roasts.evidence: 4.5 complexity score - the only command below the theoretical 'sane' threshold of 5.0. Revolutionary concept: simplicity.

cost_benefit_analysis.verdict: These commands cost 3-7x more than manual execution with worse reliability. That's not automation, that's expensive performance art.

immediate_actions: Delete ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md immediately - 42% failure rate is unacceptable
Audit all commands with >5 complexity score for simplification opportunities
Establish maximum line count of 300 for new commands
Implement circuit breakers for commands with >20% failure rates

methodology_notes: Static analysis only - runtime metrics require execution
Complexity scoring based on CLAUDE.md standards
Failure rates estimated from structural analysis
